text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Georgie, what do you think makes a good citizen? Ooh, interesting question.
zh: 我是尼尔。我是乔治。你觉得什么样的人才算是好公民呢?哦,这个问题很有意思。

text: I think a good citizen is someone who doesn't break the law and is generally kind to other people.
zh: 我认为好公民就是不违法、并且对他人一般都很友善的人。

text: What about you? Any ideas? Yeah, I agree.
zh: 你呢?有什么想法吗?对,我同意这一点。

text: But also maybe people who volunteer, so people who
zh: 但也许还应该包括那些做志愿服务的人,比如

text: run clubs for children and that kind of thing. I think they're good citizens.
zh: 为孩子们组织俱乐部之类的人。我觉得他们也是好公民。

text: Now, being a citizen, a member of society who enjoys certain rights in return for having certain responsibilities,
zh: 所谓公民,是指在享有某些权利的同时也承担相应责任的社会成员,

text: is an old idea that's been in the news a lot recently.
zh: 这是一个古老的概念,最近在新闻中被频繁提及。

text: It's something that interests Myra Anubi, presenter of BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World.
zh: BBC世界服务节目《People Fixing the World》主持人迈拉·阿努比对此很感兴趣。

text: What do you think makes a good citizen? Is it someone who follows the laws of the land and pays their taxes?
zh: 你觉得什么样的人才是好公民?是那种遵守国家法律、按时纳税的人吗?

text: Well, some people might say it should go beyond that. And a good citizen is one who tries to improve the place where they live.
zh: 也有人认为,好公民不仅要做到这些,还要努力让自己生活的地方变得更好。

text: A good citizen obeys the law of the land – a term for the set of rules and laws that exists in a country.
zh: 好公民遵守“the law of the land”,也就是一个国家现行的一整套规则和法律。

text: But should there be more to it than that? Around the world, young people today are less likely to vote.
zh: 但公民身份是否应该包含更多内容呢?如今世界各地的年轻人投票的意愿越来越低。

text: And many people aged 18 to 30 say they feel ignored by politicians.
zh: 许多18到30岁的人表示,他们感觉被政客们忽视了。

text: So in this programme, we'll be hearing about MyPolis,
zh: 所以在这期节目中,我们将了解“MyPolis”项目,

text: an exciting project helping young people in Portugal re -engage with their citizenship.
zh: 这是一个帮助葡萄牙年轻人重新参与公民生活的精彩项目。

text: And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases, all of which you'll find on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 一如既往,我们还会学习一些实用的新词汇和短语,都可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: But before that, I have a question for you, Neil.
zh: 不过在那之前,我有一个问题要考考你,尼尔。

text: The project's name, MyPolis, comes from the ancient Greek states such as Athens and Sparta, where the idea of citizenship was born.
zh: 这个项目的名字“MyPolis”来源于古希腊的城邦,比如雅典和斯巴达,公民身份的概念正是在那里诞生的。

text: So, what is the meaning of the ancient Greek word polis?
zh: 那么,古希腊语单词“polis”是什么意思呢?

text: Is it a vote, b people, or c city?
zh: 是a投票,b人民,还是c城市?

text: That's a good question. I'm going to guess b people.
zh: 这个问题不错。我猜是b人民。

text: OK, well I'll reveal the correct answer later in the programme.
zh: 好的,我会在节目稍后公布正确答案。

text: Mypolis runs council assemblies where children can propose improvements to their local school community.
zh: MyPolis组织学生议会,让孩子们提出改善本地学校社区的建议。

text: Anything from fixing broken water fountains to changing the curriculum.
zh: 从修理坏掉的饮水机到修改课程内容,什么建议都有。

text: Local politicians also attend, listening to the children's ideas and responding by giving their support or explaining why not.
zh: 当地政客也会出席,倾听孩子们的想法,并给予支持或解释为什么不能采纳。

text: The project was started by founder Bernardo Gonçalves when he was in his 20s because he was disappointed by interactions between political parties and young people.
zh: 该项目由创始人贝尔纳多·贡萨尔维斯在二十多岁时发起,因为他对政党与年轻人之间的互动感到失望。

text: Here's Bernardo telling more to BBC World Service programme People Fixing the World.
zh: 下面是贝尔纳多在BBC世界服务节目《People Fixing the World》中的讲述。

text: We have seen a quick erosion in the trust of the population
zh: 我们看到民众

text: on the democratic institutions, such as the national parliament, such as the government in office.
zh: 对民主机构,比如国家议会、比如当届政府的信任正在迅速削弱。

text: We need to find new ways to connect children and young citizens to democratic institutions.
zh: 我们需要找到新的方式,让孩子和年轻公民与民主机构建立联系。

text: Otherwise, the gap will get bigger and bigger.
zh: 否则这道鸿沟会越来越大。

text: This is very dangerous for democracy. Bernardo thinks Portugal has seen an erosion of trust,
zh: 这对民主来说非常危险。贝尔纳多认为葡萄牙出现了信任的“erosion”(侵蚀),

text: – a term for the gradual weakening of people's confidence in political and economic institutions – and the government in office.
zh: 也就是人们对政治和经济机构的信心逐渐减弱——以及对当届政府的信任。

text: A government in office means the government in power at the present time.
zh: “a government in office”指的是当前执政的政府。

text: MyPolice mirrors the political activities that children will need as adult citizens
zh: MyPolis模拟了孩子们成年后作为公民所需要的政治活动,

text: – things like voting and holding politicians accountable.
zh: 比如投票和监督政客履行职责。

text: Reporter Alison Roberts visited schools around the Portuguese capital Lisbon and was impressed by what she saw,
zh: 记者艾莉森·罗伯茨走访了葡萄牙首都里斯本周边的学校,她所见到的情形令她印象深刻,

text: as she explains here to Myra Anubi, presenter of BBC World Service's People Fixing the World.
zh: 她在BBC世界服务节目《People Fixing the World》中向主持人迈拉·阿努比讲述了这一切。

text: Another benefit is MyPolis has designed the programme
zh: 另一个好处是,MyPolis把这个项目设计得

text: so it can be included as part of the national curriculum rather than as an add -on. Well, it's good to know that this is happening in other places,
zh: 可以纳入国家课程体系,而不是作为额外附加内容。得知这种做法也在其他地方推行,真是令人欣慰。

text: but the thing that also really strikes me here is that politicians are actually listening to these kids and taking them seriously,
zh: 但更让我印象深刻的是,政客们真的在倾听这些孩子的意见,并认真对待他们,

text: giving them answers because politicians, as we know, can be hard to pin down.
zh: 给他们答复——因为我们都知道,政客通常很难被“抓住”问出实话。

text: The MyPolice programme is designed as part of the whole school curriculum and not just an add -on,
zh: MyPolis项目被设计为整个学校课程的一部分,而不仅仅是附加内容,

text: meaning something extra that is added later instead of being planned in from the start.
zh: “add-on”指的是事后额外添加、而不是从一开始就规划好的东西。

text: Myra was impressed that local politicians take the school children seriously.
zh: 迈拉对当地政客认真对待学生们印象深刻。

text: If you take someone or something seriously, you think it's important and deserves attention.
zh: 如果你“认真对待”某人或某事,意味着你认为这很重要,值得关注。

text: And that's good because, as everyone knows, politicians can be hard to pin down.
zh: 这很好,因为众所周知,政客们往往很难被“逼问”出明确答案。

text: To pin someone down means to force them to explain their plans and intentions in
zh: “to pin someone down”意思是迫使某人详细说明他们的

text: detail. It sounds like the programme is really developing active citizens of the future in
zh: 计划和意图。听起来这个项目正在真正培养葡萄牙未来的

text: Portugal. In fact, it's been so successful that similar programmes have been started in Italy, Hungary and Mozambique.
zh: 积极公民。事实上,它已经非常成功,类似的项目也已在意大利、匈牙利和莫桑比克开展。

text: OK, Neil, I think I should reveal the answer to my question.
zh: 好了,尼尔,我想是时候公布我那个问题的答案了。

text: I asked you what is the meaning of the ancient Greek word polis
zh: 我问的是古希腊语单词“polis”是什么意思,

text: and you said people, which was was the wrong answer, I'm
zh: 你回答的是“人民”,恐怕

text: afraid. The right answer was c city. Oh
zh: 这是错误答案。正确答案是c,城市。哦,

text: no! Right, it's time to recap the vocabulary we've learned,
zh: 不! 好了,是时候来回顾一下我们今天学到的词汇了,

text: starting with the law of the land, a term for the set of rules and laws that exists in a country.
zh: 首先是“the law of the land”,指一个国家现行的一整套规则和法律。

text: An erosion of trust happens when people gradually lose confidence in political and economic institutions.
zh: “an erosion of trust”指人们逐渐失去对政治和经济机构的信心。

text: If a government is in office, it means they are currently in power.
zh: 如果一个政府“in office”,意思是他们目前正在执政。

text: An add -on is an extra part that is added to something later, rather than being built in from the start.
zh: “an add-on”指事后额外添加的部分,而不是从一开始就设计好的内容。

text: If you take someone seriously, you consider them important and worthy of your attention.
zh: 如果你“take someone seriously”,意味着你认为对方很重要,值得关注。

text: And finally, if you find it hard to pin someone down, it's difficult to find out their specific plans and intentions.
zh: 最后,如果你觉得“hard to pin someone down”,意思是很难弄清楚对方具体的计划和意图。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but remember you'll find a quiz and worksheet for this episode over on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,别忘了在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上可以找到本期的测验和练习题。

text: See you there soon. Bye! Goodbye! LearningEnglish .com
zh: 我们那里见。拜拜!再见!LearningEnglish.com
